title: [lichen_l2.c:1024-1078 CRITICAL] disable path deadlocks if stop() aborts thread while it holds rx_mutex
description: Cross-file analysis: CRITICAL DEADLOCK BUG. lichen_l2_enable(state=false) at line 1024 calls lichen_lora_l2_stop(). If stop() has to abort the RX thread (lines 615-624 in lora_l2.c), it returns -ECANCELED. The disable path ignores this return value and proceeds to line 1043 where it tries to acquire rx_mutex with K_FOREVER. If the RX thread was aborted while holding rx_mutex (during lichen_l2_input callback at line 1504), the mutex is still locked by a dead thread. Attempting to acquire it causes permanent deadlock. The stop() failure return value IS captured (line 1024: int ret = stop()), but the code continues unconditionally to cleanup (line 1029-1078) regardless of ret value. The cleanup REQUIRES rx_mutex acquisition at line 1044. FIX: Check if ret == -ECANCELED before acquiring rx_mutex, and if so, call lichen_lora_l2_deinit() which reinitializes rx_mutex via lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort().
close_reason: Fixed: added -ECANCELED check after stop() to call deinit() before acquiring mutexes
